creator: dms@cwa.uk.com
text:
(In reply to Andriy Gapon from comment #3)

I understand... 9.x is a little long in the tooth now.  For the record that microserver is still in service and still running FreeBSD 9.1.  If the disks fail before the server itself, I'll swap in an uptodate version of FreeBSD. 

It's a pity I've had trouble with FreeBSD and the HP Microservers (had issues with Microserver 8 & 10 re booting, 10 was at gotten round with some community advice) as they are great budget machines.
